preamble: The elision or reduction of the diphthong [ai] to nothing is a form of extreme lenition, where the entire vowel segment is lost, typically in unstressed positions.

examples.note: Complete loss of the diphthong segment.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
